---
title: "Assignments"
toc: false
---

Public, generic versions of homework and project briefs. **Concrete
due dates, point values, and section-specific submission instructions
live in Blackboard.**

The assignment spine is designed so each piece **builds toward your
final portfolio**. By Week 15, you should be able to gather your
weekly artifacts, the LaTeX Project, the R Project, and your AI module
artifacts into a small, polished portfolio.

## Available

- [HW 1 — Setup and your first Quarto document](hw01-setup.qmd)
- [LaTeX Project — replicate a short open-access math paper](project-latex.qmd) (Week 6)
- [R Project — data analysis or simulation report](project-r.qmd) (Week 10)
- [Final Portfolio + Reflection](final-portfolio.qmd) (Week 15)

## Planned weekly briefs

Weekly briefs are added a few at a time as the course is built. The
spine is **11 weekly labs/homework** plus the two projects and the
final portfolio.

- HW 2 — Math in LaTeX (Week 2)
- HW 3 — Theorem-and-proof writeup (Week 3)
- HW 4 — Figures, tables, and a bibliography (Week 4)
- HW 5 — LaTeX Project paper pick + outline (Week 5)
- HW 7 — First R script (Week 7)
- HW 8 — A visual data story with `ggplot2` (Week 8)
- HW 9 — A reproducible simulation report (Week 9)
- HW 11 — AI debugging audit (Week 11)
- HW 12 — AI Use Reflection (Week 12)
- HW 13 — Optional second-tool bridge mini-report (Week 13)

The **best 9 of 11** weekly labs/homework count for ordinary
weekly-work credit. **HW 11 and HW 12 are the dedicated AI module
assignments in the Generative AI Literacy category and are not
droppable.** Projects, required conferences, AI verification
components, and the final portfolio are also not droppable.

## Submission

All submissions go through Blackboard. Where an assignment asks for a
file, follow the file-naming convention in the brief and submit the
exact files requested.

If a brief asks for **a rendered PDF and the `.qmd` source**, submit
both. If it asks for a `.tex` source plus the compiled PDF, submit
both.
